text:
Sure. Here's a short story.

A long time ago in a small village, there was an old crone who lived alone. Her name was Matron Helena. She lived in a cozy cottage filled with jars filled with seeds. Every morning, Helena would plant new seeds, carefully attending to each one. The village children would run past her garden.

"Helena!" they would shout.

"HELlo there," Helena would call back. "What have you got that smells nice today?"

The children would answer, "Figs!"

Helena always laughed when the children said that. Not because they weren't cute, but because Helen wished she could offer them her unique sort of fruit. Instead, Helena grew marrows.

One chilly autumn, as Helena tended to her marrows, a traveler stopped by her cottage. He was dressed warmly but looked weary.

"Ah," he sighed. "I seek shelter for the night."
